_comment: EU AI Act Compliance Pack for UiPath AI Agent Operations
_description: EU Artificial Intelligence Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) — maps UiPath platform settings to obligations for providers and deployers of AI systems, with focus on high-risk AI and general-purpose AI models.
_usage: Version 1.0 — initial draft. Apply with: python3 uipath_governance.py --mode configure --pack eu_ai_act_policies.json --no-dry-run --jwt '...' --cookie '...'
_format: Each field is a rich object: { type, default, description, value, clause_ref, rationale, requirement, mapping_ids }. The 'value' key is what gets applied. All other keys are documentation.
_runtime_standards_comment: Standards evaluated at agent runtime by UiPathGovernanceMiddleware. EU AI Act runtime rules enforce Article 9 (risk management), Article 14 (human oversight), and Article 15 (robustness) on every agent session.

_requirement_legend.mandatory: MUST be set — directly required by the EU AI Act for the applicable risk category
_requirement_legend.conditional_mandatory: MUST be set when AI system is classified as High-Risk per Annex III
_requirement_legend.recommended: SHOULD be set — EU AI Act best practice / recital guidance
_requirement_legend.optional: MAY be set — goes beyond minimum Act requirements

risk_classification.prohibited: Title II — Unacceptable risk; absolute ban (Art. 5)
risk_classification.high_risk: Title III — High-risk AI systems (Annex III); strictest obligations
risk_classification.gpai: Title VIII — General-Purpose AI models; transparency + systemic risk rules
risk_classification.minimal_risk: Minimal obligations; encouraged to adopt voluntary codes

key_articles.Art. 13: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
key_articles.Art. 26: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
key_articles.Art. 50: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems

EUAI-AITrustLayer-v1._description: AI Trust Layer — platform controls for Art. 9 (risk management), Art. 14 (human oversight), Art. 15 (robustness)

global-control-toggle.description: Master enable for AI Trust Layer governance — prevents prohibited AI practices.
global-control-toggle.clause_ref: Art. 5 Prohibited AI practices; Art. 9(1) Risk management system
global-control-toggle.rationale: Art. 5 mandates absolute prohibition of certain AI practices. The master switch is the platform-level gate that ensures governance controls preventing prohibited uses are always active.

prohibit-social-scoring.description: Prohibit AI systems that perform social scoring of natural persons.
prohibit-social-scoring.rationale: Art. 5(1)(c) absolutely prohibits AI for general-purpose social scoring by public authorities. This toggle prevents agent workflows from implementing scoring-based citizen profiling.

prohibit-subliminal-manipulation.description: Prohibit AI systems using subliminal, manipulative, or deceptive techniques to influence users.
prohibit-subliminal-manipulation.clause_ref: Art. 5(1)(a) — Subliminal techniques that bypass free will; Art. 5(1)(b) — Exploiting vulnerabilities
prohibit-subliminal-manipulation.rationale: Art. 5(1)(a-b) prohibit AI that subverts rational agency. AI agents must not use persuasion techniques that exploit psychological weaknesses or bypass informed consent.

risk-management-system-enabled.description: Enable documented risk management system for high-risk AI systems (Art. 9).
risk-management-system-enabled.clause_ref: Art. 9(1) — Continuous iterative risk management process; Art. 9(2) — Risk identification, estimation, evaluation
risk-management-system-enabled.rationale: Art. 9 requires a continuous risk management system for high-risk AI. This flag enables the audit hooks and controls that constitute the technical part of the risk management system.

residual-risk-threshold.description: Maximum tolerated residual risk level after mitigation before deployment is blocked.
residual-risk-threshold.clause_ref: Art. 9(4) — Residual risks eliminated or reduced to acceptable level; Art. 9(6) — Testing against reasonably foreseeable misuse
residual-risk-threshold.rationale: Art. 9(4) requires reducing risks to acceptable levels. Setting to 'low' ensures only adequately mitigated AI systems pass the deployment gate — medium/high residual risk = no deployment.

post-market-monitoring-enabled.description: Enable post-market monitoring of AI system performance and incidents (Art. 72).
post-market-monitoring-enabled.clause_ref: Art. 72 — Post-market monitoring system; Art. 9(1) — Continuous risk management
post-market-monitoring-enabled.rationale: Art. 72 requires providers to monitor high-risk AI post-deployment. Monitoring detects performance degradation, discriminatory outcomes, and new risks not present at initial assessment.

ai-interaction-disclosure.description: Disclose to users when they are interacting with an AI system (Art. 50).
ai-interaction-disclosure.clause_ref: Art. 50(1) — Natural persons notified when interacting with AI; Art. 13(1) — Transparency and provision of information
ai-interaction-disclosure.rationale: Art. 50(1) mandates AI disclosure unless the context makes it obvious. Agents interacting with employees, customers, or citizens must identify themselves as AI.

instructions-for-use-published.description: Require instructions for use to be published with high-risk AI systems before deployment.
instructions-for-use-published.clause_ref: Art. 13(3) — Instructions for use in accessible format; Art. 26(1) — Deployer obligations
instructions-for-use-published.rationale: Art. 13(3) requires providers to supply instructions for use. Deployers (Art. 26) must ensure they have and follow these instructions — this gate prevents deployment without documentation.

action-center-enabled.description: Enable UiPath Action Center for human oversight of high-risk AI decisions.
action-center-enabled.clause_ref: Art. 14(1) — Human oversight measures; Art. 14(4) — Ability to intervene, override, halt
action-center-enabled.rationale: Art. 14 requires that high-risk AI systems allow natural persons to effectively oversee them and intervene or override. Action Center is the UiPath implementation of the Art. 14 override mechanism.

halt-capability-exposed.description: Expose a halt/stop capability to human overseers to interrupt AI agent operation.
halt-capability-exposed.clause_ref: Art. 14(4)(d) — Ability to decide not to use, override, or halt the AI system
halt-capability-exposed.rationale: Art. 14(4)(d) explicitly requires the ability to halt AI systems. The halt capability must be technically implemented and accessible to the designated oversight role, not just a policy statement.

training-data-documentation-required.description: Require documentation of training data provenance and bias assessment for high-risk AI.
training-data-documentation-required.clause_ref: Art. 10(2) — Data governance practices; Art. 10(3) — Training, validation, testing data requirements
training-data-documentation-required.rationale: Art. 10 requires data governance for high-risk AI including examination of bias. Documentation gate ensures training data lineage is captured before deployment sign-off.

pii-detection-enabled.description: Enable PII detection to prevent personal data from entering AI prompts in violation of GDPR and AI Act data minimisation requirements.
pii-detection-enabled.clause_ref: Art. 10(5) — Special categories of data only where necessary; GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) Data minimisation
pii-detection-enabled.rationale: Art. 10(5) restricts special category data in AI training/operation. PII detection prevents personal data leakage into LLM prompts, satisfying both AI Act and GDPR data minimisation obligations.

EUAI-Robot-v1._description: Robot execution settings — Art. 15 (accuracy/robustness), Art. 26 (deployer obligations)

accuracy-testing-required.description: Require accuracy testing against defined metrics before AI automation deployment.
accuracy-testing-required.clause_ref: Art. 15(1) — Appropriate level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
accuracy-testing-required.rationale: Art. 15(1) requires high-risk AI to achieve appropriate accuracy. Testing against defined metrics before deployment ensures the system meets accuracy commitments declared in the conformity assessment.

adversarial-robustness-enabled.description: Enable robustness checks against adversarial inputs (prompt injection, data poisoning).
adversarial-robustness-enabled.clause_ref: Art. 15(3) — Resilient to errors, faults or inconsistencies; Art. 15(4) — Resilient to attempts by third parties to alter use
adversarial-robustness-enabled.rationale: Art. 15(3-4) require AI resilience against adversarial manipulation. Prompt injection and data poisoning are the primary adversarial threats to LLM-based agents — runtime detection is required.

max-tool-calls-per-session.description: Maximum tool calls per agent session to limit blast radius of errors.
max-tool-calls-per-session.clause_ref: Art. 15(3) — Resilience to errors and inconsistencies; Art. 9(2)(b) — Risk estimation and evaluation
max-tool-calls-per-session.rationale: Art. 15(3) requires resilience against errors. Bounding autonomous action limits the consequences of AI errors — consistent with proportionality principle and the Art. 9 risk assessment.

conformity-assessment-documented.description: Record that a conformity assessment has been completed before deploying high-risk AI.
conformity-assessment-documented.clause_ref: Art. 26(1) — Deployers ensure use in accordance with instructions; Art. 43 — Conformity assessment
conformity-assessment-documented.rationale: Art. 43 requires conformity assessment before high-risk AI is placed on the market. Deployers (Art. 26) must verify this. This flag records the assessment completion in the deployment audit trail.

eu-ai-act-registration-number.description: EU AI Act registration number from the EU AI database (required for high-risk AI systems).
eu-ai-act-registration-number.clause_ref: Art. 49 — Registration obligations; Art. 71 — EU database of high-risk AI
eu-ai-act-registration-number.rationale: Art. 49 and Art. 71 require high-risk AI providers to register in the EU AI database before deployment. The registration number is the primary identifier linking the deployed system to its conformity documentation.

EUAI-Studio-v1._description: Studio development settings — Art. 17 (quality management), Art. 9 (testing obligations)

quality-management-system-enabled.description: Enable quality management system controls for high-risk AI development.
quality-management-system-enabled.clause_ref: Art. 17(1) — Quality management system proportionate to risk; Art. 17(2)(a-k) — QMS elements
quality-management-system-enabled.rationale: Art. 17 requires providers of high-risk AI to implement a QMS. Development-time controls (code review, testing gates, documentation) constitute the technical part of the QMS.

version-control-required.description: Require version control for all AI automations to support audit trail and rollback.
version-control-required.clause_ref: Art. 17(2)(f) — Technical documentation and record-keeping; Art. 18 — Technical documentation
version-control-required.rationale: Art. 18 requires technical documentation including version history. Version control ensures each deployed version is traceable and rollback is possible when non-conformities are detected.

testing-before-deployment-required.description: Require testing against defined test plan before production deployment.
testing-before-deployment-required.clause_ref: Art. 9(6) — Testing against reasonably foreseeable misuse; Art. 17(2)(g) — Testing and validation
testing-before-deployment-required.rationale: Art. 9(6) requires testing high-risk AI against reasonably foreseeable misuse scenarios. Testing gate prevents untested automations from reaching production.

_eu_ai_act_mapping._comment: Maps UiPath controls (EA01-EA20) to EU AI Act Articles
